name: Bactrosaurus
tagline: One of the earliest duckbills, with hundreds of crushing teeth packed into powerful jaws.
intro: Bactrosaurus was one of the first hadrosaurs or "duckbilled dinosaurs." This family of dinosaurs gets its "duckbill" name from its wide, toothless beak. Bactrosaurus's beak had a horny covering and was used to bite off twigs and leaves. Measuring only about twenty feet in length, Bactrosaurus was small for a hadrosaur.

topics.title: Grinding and Grating
topics.text: Duckbilled dinosaurs had jaws made for grinding and crushing plants just as cows and horses do today. Unlike cows and horses, however, duckbilled dinosaurs did not chew by moving their jaws from one side to the other. Instead, when hadrosaurs closed their jaws, both sides of the upper jaw pushed outward so that the upper teeth ground against the lower teeth, crushing the food in between.

captions.label: A multitude of teeth
captions.text: Rows of overlapping teeth packed into great plant-grinding surfaces easily reduced twigs, fruits, and seeds to a soft pulp.
